heading: Withdrawal of Federal recognition
label: 323 Withdrawal of Federal recognition
notes: Historical and Revision Notes1956ActRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)323(a)323(b)323(c)32:154 (last 26 words of last par.).32:115 (1st sentence, and 1st 24 words of 2d sentence).50:1116 (last 15 words of 1st sentence).(1st sentence, and 1st 24 words of 2d sentence); restated (1st sentence, and 1st 24 words of 2d sentence), .June 3, 1916, ch. 134, § 76June 15, 1933, ch. 87, § 1348 Stat. 158(last 25 words of last par.); restated (last 137 words of last par.); restated (last 25 words), .June 3, 1916, ch. 134, § 110Sept. 22, 1922, ch. 423, § 6May 12, 1928, ch. 52945 Stat. 501(last 15 words of 1st sentence), .July 9, 1952, ch. 608, § 70666 Stat. 503In subsection (a) the words “ceases to have the qualifications prescribed under ” are substituted for 32:154 (last 26 words of last par.), since it is implicit that a member who could not be paid would lose his federally recognized status (see JAGA 1953/9033, 3 Dec. 1953). The last 23 words of subsection (a) are inserted as a necessary implication of the rule stated in .section 300 of this titlesection 309(c) of this titleIn subsection (b), the words “or warrant officer” are omitted, since defines “officer” to include warrant officers. The word “detailed” is substituted for the word “appointed”, since the filling of the positions involved is not appointment to an office in the constitutional sense. The word “commissioned” is inserted after the words “composed of”, since the word “officer” alone, in 32:115, referred to a commissioned officer only (see opinion of the Judge Advocate General of the Army (JAGA 1953/4078, 6 May 1953)). The words “who outrank him” are substituted for the words “senior in rank to the officer under investigation”.section 101(9) of this titleIn subsection (c), the opening clause is substituted for the words “such transfer”. The words “his Federal recognition is withdrawn” are substituted for the words “shall terminate his federally recognized National Guard or Air National Guard status”.

(e)(2) (78th through 85th words), , 1181.Sept. 3, 1954, ch. 125768 Stat. 1161The change [in subsec. (b)(1) and (2)] is necessary to exclude from the efficiency board commissioned officers of the Army Reserve or Air Force Reserve, in accordance with the source law, the first sentence of , chapter 134 (formerly (1st sentence)).section 76 of the Act of June 3, 191632 U.S.C. 115In subsection (d), the words “notwithstanding ” are omitted as surplusage.section 115 of title 32In subsection (e), the words “if appropriate” are omitted as surplusage.

Amendments1994—Subsec. (c). , substituted “12105, 12213(a), or 12214(a)” for “3259, 3352(a), 8259, or 8352(a)”.Pub. L. 103–337, § 1676(a)(3)Subsecs. (d), (e). , added subsec. (d) and struck out former subsecs. (d) and (e) which read as follows:Pub. L. 103–337, § 1630(2)“(d) Except as provided in sections 1005 and 1006 of title 10, the Federal recognition of a second lieutenant of the Army National Guard who is discharged under for failure of promotion shall be withdrawn on the date of that discharge.section 3820(c) of title 10“(e) Except as provided in sections 1005 and 1006 of title 10, the Federal recognition of a reserve officer of the Air Force who is not recommended for promotion under section 8368(c)(1) or (2) of title 10, or who is found to be not qualified for Federal recognition under section 8368(d) or (e) of title 10, shall be withdrawn.”1958—Subsec. (b)(1). , substituted “the Regular Army or the Army National Guard of the United States, or both” for “a regular or reserve component of the Army”.Pub. L. 85–861, § 33(c)(2)Subsec. (b)(2). , substituted “the Regular Air Force or the Air National Guard of the United States, or both” for “a regular or reserve component of the Air Force”.Pub. L. 85–861, § 33(c)(2)Subsecs. (d), (e). , added subsecs. (d) and (e).Pub. L. 85–861, § 2(11)

Suspension of Subsection (d) of This SectionFor authority of the President to suspend subsec. (d) of this section in time of war or emergency declared by Congress, see .section 111 of this title

children.label: a
children.text: Whenever a member of the National Guard ceases to have the qualifications prescribed under or ceases to be a member of a federally recognized unit or organization of the National Guard, his Federal recognition shall be withdrawn.section 301 of this title
the Regular Army or the Army National Guard of the United States, or both, who out-rank him and who are detailed by the Secretary of the Army, if he is a member of the Army National Guard; or
the Regular Air Force or the Air National Guard of the United States, or both, who outrank him and who are detailed by the Secretary of the Air Force, if he is a member of the Air National Guard.

children.label: c
children.text: If a member of the Army National Guard of the United States or the Air National Guard of the United States is transferred to the Army Reserve or the Air Force Reserve, as the case may be, under section 12105, 12213(a), or 12214(a) of title 10, his Federal recognition is withdrawn.
federally recognized as an officer of the National Guard; and
subject to involuntary transfer to the Retired Reserve, transfer to an inactive status list, or discharge under chapter 1407, 1409, or 1411 of title 10;
